description: Machine is the Schema for the machines API.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

connection.description: Connection contains connection data for a Baseboard Management Controller.

authSecretRef.description:
AuthSecretRef is the SecretReference that contains authentication information of the Machine.
The Secret must contain username and password keys. This is optional as it is not required when using
the RPC provider.

name.description: name is unique within a namespace to reference a secret resource.

namespace.description: namespace defines the space within which the secret name must be unique.

host.description: Host is the host IP address or hostname of the Machine.

port.description: Port is the port number for connecting with the Machine.

intelAMT.description: IntelAMT contains the options to customize the IntelAMT provider.

hostScheme.description: HostScheme determines whether to use http or https for intelAMT calls.

port.description: Port that intelAMT will use for calls.

ipmitool.description: IPMITOOL contains the options to customize the Ipmitool provider.

cipherSuite.description: CipherSuite that ipmitool will use for calls.

port.description: Port that ipmitool will use for calls.

preferredOrder.description:
PreferredOrder allows customizing the order that BMC providers are called.
Providers added to this list will be moved to the front of the default order.
Provider names are case insensitive.
The default order is: ipmitool, asrockrack, gofish, intelamt, dell, supermicro, openbmc.

items.description: ProviderName is the bmclib specific provider name. Names are case insensitive.

redfish.description: Redfish contains the options to customize the Redfish provider.

port.description: Port that redfish will use for calls.

systemName.description:
SystemName is the name of the system to use for redfish calls.
With redfish implementations that manage multiple systems via a single endpoint, this allows for specifying the system to manage.

useBasicAuth.description: UseBasicAuth for redfish calls. The default is false which means token based auth is used.

rpc.description: RPC contains the options to customize the RPC provider.

consumerURL.description:
ConsumerURL is the URL where an rpc consumer/listener is running
and to which we will send and receive all notifications.

dotPath.description: DotPath is the path to the json object where the bmclib RequestPayload{} struct will be embedded. For example: object.data.body

hmac.description: HMAC is the options used to create a HMAC signature.

prefixSigDisabled.description: PrefixSigDisabled determines whether the algorithm will be prefixed to the signature. Example: sha256=abc123

items.description:
SecretReference represents a Secret Reference. It has enough information to retrieve secret
in any namespace

name.description: name is unique within a namespace to reference a secret resource.

namespace.description: namespace defines the space within which the secret name must be unique.

secrets.description: Secrets are a map of algorithms to secrets used for signing.

logNotificationsDisabled.description: LogNotificationsDisabled determines whether responses from rpc consumer/listeners will be logged or not.

request.description: Request is the options used to create the rpc HTTP request.

httpContentType.description: HTTPContentType is the content type to use for the rpc request notification.

httpMethod.description: HTTPMethod is the HTTP method to use for the rpc request notification.

staticHeaders.description: StaticHeaders are predefined headers that will be added to every request.

timestampFormat.description: TimestampFormat is the time format for the timestamp header.

timestampHeader.description: TimestampHeader is the header name that should contain the timestamp. Example: X-BMCLIB-Timestamp

signature.description: Signature is the options used for adding an HMAC signature to an HTTP request.

appendAlgoToHeaderDisabled.description:
AppendAlgoToHeaderDisabled decides whether to append the algorithm to the signature header or not.
Example: X-BMCLIB-Signature becomes X-BMCLIB-Signature-256
When set to true, a header will be added for each algorithm. Example: X-BMCLIB-Signature-256 and X-BMCLIB-Signature-512

headerName.description: HeaderName is the header name that should contain the signature(s). Example: X-BMCLIB-Signature

includedPayloadHeaders.description:
IncludedPayloadHeaders are headers whose values will be included in the signature payload. Example: X-BMCLIB-My-Custom-Header
All headers will be deduplicated.

status.description: MachineStatus defines the observed state of Machine.

conditions.description: Conditions represents the latest available observations of an object's current state.

items.description: MachineCondition defines an observed condition of a Machine.

message.description: Message is a human readable message indicating with details of the last transition.

powerState.description: Power is the current power state of the Machine.
